architecture.summary: AtlasKV augments frozen LLMs with billion-scale knowledge graphs by converting KG triples into learned key-value (KV) pairs injected into transformer self-attention layers. The system consists of two components: (1) KG2KV, which converts each KG triple into query-key-value training data by masking entities and rewriting relations into noun forms, then encoding these strings with a sentence encoder; (2) HiKVP, a hierarchical pruning algorithm that organizes KG keys into a 3-layer tree (using UMAP + GMM clustering), stores most data on CPU, and performs multi-stage top-k filtering during inference to select relevant KV pairs for GPU-based attention computation. Information flows from KG triples → Q-K-V strings → sentence embeddings → hierarchical clustering → (at inference) multi-layer pruning with CPU/GPU offloading → pruned KV injection into LLM attention heads.
trained_components: KG projection heads for key-value embedding transformation
Sentence encoder (frozen pre-trained model, weights not updated in AtlasKV training)

biblio.title: AtlasKV: Augmenting LLMs with Billion-Scale Knowledge Graphs in 20GB VRAM

claims.statement: AtlasKV requires less than 20GB VRAM to augment LLMs with 1 billion KG triples, compared to over 40GB VRAM for KBLaM with 100K triples
AtlasKV achieves Top-1 accuracy of 87.3% on ATLAS-Pes2o-QKV with 1M triples (128 pruning), compared to KBLaM's 25.5% at 3K training steps
KG2KV achieves a diversity ratio of 7.864% compared to synthetic method's 0.003% on training data enquiry attributes
KG2KV requires average token cost of 165.7 per triple compared to synthetic method's 349.9
AtlasKV achieves GPT-4o relevance score of approximately 0.85–0.90 on multiple datasets, outperforming KBLaM's scores below 0.75
AtlasKV achieves sub-linear time complexity O(k·log N) and memory complexity O(k·log N) during inference, compared to KBLaM's linear O(N) where N is KG size and k is pruning budget
AtlasKV requires only 3K–20K training steps (on ATLAS-Wiki-QKV) compared to KBLaM's 20K steps, and generalizes to OOD datasets (ATLAS-Pes2o-QKV, ATLAS-CC-QKV) without retraining

AtlasKV with HiKVP pruning (128-64-16) shows non-negligible performance drop compared to AtlasKV without HiKVP (e.g., ACC@1 from 92.7% to 87.3% on 1M triples, ATLAS-Pes2o-QKV), although authors attribute this to model's ability to perform fuzzy retrieval across hierarchical layers
